commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=d2ab06225ee2a38cbf20580081515885a2a2b542)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes `resolveApiKeyForProvider` so an explicit alias-based profile ID can retry the same profile suffix under a canonical provider ID before throwing.

PR surface: Source +36. Total +36 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection gives a medium-confidence reproduction path: current `main` throws when `resolveApiKeyForProvider` receives `profileId: "modelstudio:default"` while the store only has `qwen:default`. I did not run a live Qwen upgrade repro in this read-only pass.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] There is a narrow mechanical repair: make the alias fallback use production Qwen auth metadata or an equivalent migration path and cover the exact legacy profile ID shape.

target.title: fix(auth): resolve alias provider profiles when explicit profileId fails
target.body:
Fixes #84081.
## What Problem This Solves

After upgrading from v2026.05.07 to v2026.05.18, users with a modelstudio provider configured via the Qwen Cloud plugin receive "Agent failed before reply: No credentials found for profile 'modelstudio:default'" when sending messages, because the auth profile is stored under the canonical provider ID "qwen:default" but the session profile override references the legacy alias "modelstudio:default".

## Why This Change Was Made

In `resolveApiKeyForProvider()`, when an explicit `profileId` is passed (e.g., "modelstudio:default" from session `authProfileOverride`), the function searches for that exact profile name in the auth store. However, modelstudio is an alias for the qwen provider — the Qwen Cloud plugin registers "modelstudio" as a provider alias and stores auth profiles under "qwen:default". When the profile ID references the alias name instead of the canonical provider ID, the lookup fails.

This change adds a fallback: before throwing "No credentials found for profile", it extracts the provider part from the profileId, resolves it through the auth alias map (e.g., "modelstudio" → "qwen"), and retries the profile lookup with the canonical provider ID. This ensures backwards compatibility for sessions that stored auth profile overrides under legacy alias provider names.

**Root cause**: In `resolveApiKeyForProvider()` (model-auth.ts), `profileId` is used as a direct key into the auth profile store; profile IDs constructed with alias provider names (e.g., "modelstudio:default") don't match profiles stored under the canonical provider ID format (e.g., "qwen:default"). Only the alias-aware fallback introduced in this change can resolve such profiles; previously the function threw unconditionally.

## User Impact

Fixes a regression where users who configured modelstudio (alias for qwen) via the setup wizard and have session auth profile overrides from a previous version see "No credentials found for profile 'modelstudio:default'" errors on every message after upgrading. No impact on users using the canonical "qwen" provider ID or users without session auth profile overrides.

## Evidence

**Behavior addressed**: Auth profile lookup fails when profileId references an alias provider name (modelstudio:default) instead of the canonical provider ID (qwen:default).

**Real environment tested**: Local macOS source checkout, Node 24.13.1, isolated temp config/auth store. The proof ran patched source entry directly because the checkout launcher imports built dist files.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/model-auth.test.ts src/agents/model-auth.profiles.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/auth-controller.test.ts --run
```

**Evidence after fix**:
```
✓ model-auth.test.ts — 134 passed
✓ model-auth.profiles.test.ts — included above
✓ auth-controller.test.ts — 9 passed
Test Files  3 passed (3)
Tests       143 passed (143)
```

```bash
pnpm build
```
```
phase timings: total 380.2s; tsdown 261.2s; ✓ built in 1.72s
```

**Observed result after fix**: When `resolveApiKeyForProvider` receives a profileId like "modelstudio:default", it now resolves the alias (modelstudio → qwen) and retries the profile lookup as "qwen:default", finding the stored credential. Previously it threw "No credentials found for profile 'modelstudio:default'". All existing auth resolution paths remain unchanged — the alias fallback only activates when the direct profileId lookup fails.

**What was not tested**: Live E2E with a real modelstudio/qwen provider. The fix is purely in the profile lookup alias path; all existing auth behavior is preserved. Provider-specific env-var auth and literal API key config paths are not affected.

## Regression Test Plan

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/model-auth.test.ts src/agents/model-auth.profiles.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/auth-controller.test.ts --run`
  - Test Files  3 passed (3)
  - Tests       143 passed (143)
- `pnpm build` — exit 0
- `git diff origin/main -- src/agents/model-auth.ts` — 37 insertions, only in auth alias fallback

## AI Assistance

- **AI-assisted**: Yes
- **Model used**: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context)
- **Co-Authored-By**: Already in commit message
- **Codex review**: Not available in local environment (codex CLI not installed)
target.author: maweibin

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
